<<FILL role=ending words=90 beats='At the crest, in the last light, the place where she crossed shows its single line of prints along the cornice edge, laid exactly where the wind has packed the snow to bearing, a route no guild book holds; an old queen's private pass; Rill memorises its shape against the sky the way Maren taught, by heart, and leaves the prints to the wind, and carries the route home unwritten'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The wax bench in the harness room, runners tipped up and dressed the guild way, lashings gone over inch by inch, Bracken's paws checked pad by pad while he leans his whole opinionated weight into the work; small unhurried maintenance, the kind Maren called the cheapest rescue there is, the one you do before anything goes wrong'>>

choices.label: Back out to the route board.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Out of the yard on the Ashfirth line, the guild steps dropping to the fell road and the larch valley opening below in greys and whites; the pass-ledger snug in its oilcloth, the day's cold taking a first honest bite; write this re-entry-safe, the road out is the same road however many legs the winter has already held'>>

choices.label: Down the fell road to where the Ashfirth ways divide.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='The river-ice line under the alder banks, level as a table and quick as a whistle, the guild's daylight-only road; the ice talks its small settling talk and the alders lean over rimed to the last twig; wand to wand the valley slides by at double pace, the sledge running so sweet it hums'>>

choices.label: Hold the fast line all the way down to the mill weir.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The alder narrows, where the banks pinch and the wands cross to the shadier side; otter slides groove the snow down every bank like children's chutes, and one set of bigger prints crosses the river in a straight unhurried line, older than this morning, going north; the weir's rail shows ahead through the trees'>>

choices.label: On to the weir and up toward the village.
Pause at the otter slides a moment, because some things insist.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Two otters flow down their slide and across the ice like poured water, utterly unbothered by winter, dog, or guild business; Bracken quivers with professional interest and holds the line because he is a working dog and says so with his whole rigid body; thirty seconds of pure valley, spent well'>>

choices.label: Back to the line and on to the weir.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The wand-count taken up the drilled way, fifty paces, flag, fifty paces, flag, the rhythm that keeps a line honest when the light goes flat; two wands lean, one is buried to its ribbon, and the drift is still building off the shoulder as the wind sorts itself for weather'>>

choices.label: Stop at the buried stretch and take stock.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The wood's hush deepens past the first hundred paces, the boughs roofed with a winter's weight, the lamp-ring the only moving thing; somewhere off the line a bough sheds its load with a soft whump and the silence closes back over the sound like water; the compass needle sits steady as a kept promise'>>

choices.label: Settle into the count and follow the needle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The lane up from the weir between snow walls shoulder-high, the village sounds arriving one by one, a dog, an axe, a door; write this entry-agnostic, by road, wood, or river the lane is the same climbing lane, and the gate lanterns show at its top like two low kind stars'>>

choices.label: Up to the gate lanterns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Up from the mill by the short path, past the sheeted millstones and the ice-locked wheel with its beard of icicles, joining the village lane where it crests; the gate lanterns are lit ahead and somewhere behind them a smith's hammer is keeping easy time with the evening'>>

choices.label: Join the lane at the crest and go up to the gate.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The gate brazier's circle of warmth, mittens steaming on the rail, a couple of village elders already installed on the bench like punctuation; Sorrel feeds the fire a knot of root and the sparks go up among the first stars; a good place for a story, and she knows one is owed'>>

choices.label: Settle by the brazier and ask for the true telling.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The trough ice cracked and hauled out in grey slabs, the pump worked till it remembers its job, the beasts shouldering in to drink in their steaming order; Piet keeps up a commentary on every ewe by name and grievance; plain barn-warm work, and the day's cold left outside the door where it belongs'>>

choices.label: Finish up and find Sorrel at the hall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The village hall fire built man-high, the gathered-in households sorting themselves along the benches with quilts and gossip; on the settle the oldest voices are already comparing this winter to the wolf-year winters of their childhoods, and not one of them sounds afraid; deep cold outside, deep talk inside, the village's oldest arithmetic'>>

choices.label: Through to Sorrel's table and the sealed answers.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The send-off at the gate, Sorrel's seal pressed warm into the last wax, the oilcloth packet knotted into the crate the guild way; she puts one hand flat on the sledge rail a moment, the hearth-keeper's blessing, which is not a word but a weight, and steps back into her lantern light'>>

choices.label: Take the line away from Ashfirth.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Round to the stillroom yard under the guild wall, where the Scarpsett line starts its long look upward; the crag stands over the valley in grey ledges with the switchback wands stitched across it like tacking thread; write this re-entry-safe, the yard is the same cold anteroom whatever the winter has already held'>>

choices.label: In to the stillroom bench.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The lower turns with the load settling into its lashings, the sledge finding its new balance, Bracken adjusting his haul to the weight the way a good dog does, without being asked; the valley drops away a ledge at a time and the wind starts taking an interest'>>

choices.label: On up to where the fourth turn should be.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Partway to the fork the carriers' basket passes overhead on its long cable, two figures at the crag top paying it down hand over hand to meet the chest at the stillroom, the whole apparatus creaking like a patient animal; word sent, word answered, the crag already working; there is a particular pleasure in watching a plan become moving parts'>>

choices.label: Climb on to the fork under the crag.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The run between gust-sets, counted and committed, three turns taken at a working trot with the wind drawing breath; the third gust arrives early and leans on sledge and runner like a tired horse, and then lets go, and the saddle's notch is suddenly there ahead, full of sky'>>

choices.label: Through the notch onto the saddle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The railed gallery walk up the last of the crag, iron cold even through mitts, the valley swinging below in slow grey-white panels; the rail rings faintly in the wind, one long patient note, and the saddle opens overhead like a door standing ajar'>>

choices.label: Up the last rail-length onto the saddle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The great bend of the shelf road, where the whole southern fells unroll below in ranked white ridges and the road hangs its easy curve over all of it; Maren used to halt loads here a breath, not for rest, she said, for bookkeeping, to count what the work was for; the sledge runs the curve smooth as a signature'>>

choices.label: Round the bend and on to the saddle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The terrace steps down into the village's windbreak, snow packed to a polish and gritted with hearth-ash the Scarpsett way, houses rising on either hand with their backs to the crag; the fever house lamp grows from a yellow point to a yellow window as the steps go down'>>

choices.label: Down the last flight to the gate.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Along the terrace street, doors opening a hand's width as the guild patch passes, word travelling indoors faster than boots travel outdoors; an old man sweeping his step stands straight and touches his cap brim, which is Scarpsett for a speech of welcome; the fever house door is scrubbed and waiting'>>

choices.label: To the fever house porch.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The counting-in, bottle by bottle against Iben's ledger, fever-bark, willow, salve, each label read aloud twice and each cork checked, the winter's medicine made certain the same way a route is made certain, mark by mark; Iben initials the page and allows that the guild teaches reading properly, which is a decoration'>>

choices.label: Ledger squared: back to the ward.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The miller's girl arranges herself to sleep facing her window and the crag, in the way of someone keeping an appointment; her whisper follows the tray out, if you see the White Wolf, tell her our window is the one with the blue jug; the ward settles, cot by cot, into the deep mending quiet'>>

choices.label: Draw the door to and step back to the dispensary.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The climb back to the saddle through the dusk-blue terraces, lamps coming on below step by step as the village banks its fires; at the saddle notch the wind has dropped the way it does at day's end, as if the mountain too were drawing breath before night; the line home runs down pale and sure'>>

choices.label: Over the saddle and onto the line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Down the steps behind the carriers' hand-barrow, which descends with the ceremony of a small parade, the boy running ahead announcing everything twice; write this neutral to what the sledge or basket carries, the steps and the welcome are the same; Scarpsett does not get many visitors in deep winter and means to enjoy this one'>>

choices.label: Down to the gate with the parade.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='The river road down from the guild yard, the valley flattening toward Drumford and the river showing at last, a broad white way between its black alder banks with the broken bridge mid-stream like a bitten seam; write this re-entry-safe, the road down is the same road whatever the winter has already held'>>

choices.label: Down to the near bank and the watch hut smoke.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Close in along the bank the pressure ridges show their work, plates of ice thick as tabletops shouldered up on end where the river fought the cold and lost loudly; the fallen span sits wedged among them, sprung but whole, recoverable at thaw if the ice lets it down kindly; a reading worth exact words in Nan's book'>>

choices.label: Take the reading in to the watch hut.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The bore-holes drilled at Nan's marked stations, the auger biting down to water and the tape dropped in, thickness called and chalked, hand-spans of good grey ice under the flagged line and the one thin lens circled in red; the ice-book takes the day's truth in Nan's small columns, which is how a river gets a memory'>>

choices.label: Auger stowed: back to the hut and the day's list.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The boy with the skates argues his case the whole way down the row, cites precedent, offers character witnesses, and is overruled by every grown-up in earshot with the fondness of people who all once made the same argument; he settles for carrying the skates importantly, laces over his shoulder like a bandolier'>>

choices.label: Gather the last crossers at the bank.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Nan sets the crossing order at the bank like a woman dealing cards she has dealt all her life, barrow first to prove the line, baskets in the middle, light feet last, the guild's runner and dog on the flank where eyes do the most good; everyone repeats their place back to her because that is the toll of her bridge'>>

choices.label: Lead off onto the flagged line in order.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The dogleg taken wand by close-set wand around the red-ringed lens, the line bending like an elbow exactly as flagged, the pole tapping ring, ring, ring; somebody behind hums to keep their feet in rhythm and somebody else takes it up, and the crossing acquires a tune, as crossings do when they are going well'>>

choices.label: Straighten out mid-river and carry on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The millrace hut at the plank-line's end, wheel frozen mid-turn under a fringe of icicles like a stopped clock; the race mutters underneath about the spring it is planning; from the hut's corner the far-bank lane shows plain, and the plank-line's little thrill settles into the ordinary pleasure of arriving'>>

choices.label: Up the rise into the lane.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The island's tail, where the lee shore lets go and the open river takes over for the last stretch, the lamp-ring paling as the sky's grey light comes back; a heron stands hunched at the ice edge like a folded umbrella, disapproving of everything, and does not trouble itself to move for dog or sledge'>>

choices.label: Cross the last open stretch to the far bank.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The far-bank lane between its snow walls, doors already opening along it, and the crossing's arrival travelling ahead by that wireless every village runs on; write this entry-agnostic, by main line, plank, or island road the lane receives everyone the same, with lamplight and names called and the smell of bread let out of doorways on purpose'>>

choices.label: Up to Mardy's gable lamp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The cat, released, makes one circuit of Mardy's kitchen at the speed of rumour, inspects Bracken and finds him adequate, and takes the hearth chair with the finality of a flag planted on a summit; write this neutral to who or what came over the ice, the kitchen's welcome is the same warm occupation either way'>>

choices.label: Surrender to the kettle ceremony.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The returners gathered under the gable lamp, counted and quilted, a hand-barrow of far-bank wool for the near side because freight, like rivers, runs both ways; Nan's three line-lanterns show out on the ice, patient as buoys; the back-crossing forms up in the blue end of the light'>>

choices.label: Out onto the line for the back-crossing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='The fell road north out of the yard, the quiet quarter of the fells where Maren's old line runs; the road carries no fresh sledge marks, nobody comes this way since her caches stopped being kept, and the snow lies unopened like a letter; write this re-entry-safe, the road out is the same road whatever the winter has already held'>>

choices.label: Along to the Thornmere stile.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The page turned over, and on its back a sketch in Maren's spare pencil, the Queen's Ridge drawn from this very stile, its crest of worked stones picked out carefully, and beneath it three words underlined twice, older than the routes; she never sketched anything she did not think a wayfinder would one day need to recognise'>>

choices.label: Fold the page away and take up the line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The first pine, a modest tree with a modest tin, polished like all the rest and empty like all the rest, the peg worn silky with openings; beneath it one older bed pressed in the needles' shelter, used this winter but not lately; the line's story is starting to read like rounds kept by something patient, oldest trees first'>>

choices.label: Back to the line and on toward the gully.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='A boundary cairn at the moor's edge, winter-toppled, its top stones scattered dark on the snow; ten minutes' cold masonry sets it right, the guild habit that says a route is everyone's furniture; the raven supervises from a rock with its collar ruffed, making remarks'>>

choices.label: Take the open moor stretch behind the mended cairn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='A white hare breaks from the heather almost underfoot and pours away across the moor, and Bracken, mid-stride, wants it with every hair and holds the line anyway, shivering with virtue; Maren's training two winters deep in him still; the tracks alongside the route never waver toward the hare line either, which is its own quiet information'>>

choices.label: On along the line to the second pine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Away from the seep the moor's light does its winter trick, the low sun catching the blowing ground-snow so the whole surface smokes gold at ankle height; the big calm prints hold beside the buried line through all of it, and for a hundred paces runner, dog, and absent wolf all keep the same old road'>>

choices.label: Follow the golden smoke to the second pine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Mid-tarn the ice rings underfoot like a struck bell, the deep bearing note Maren wrote of, and down in the black glass an oak leaf hangs frozen mid-sink, gold as a coin in three fathoms of dark; the far shore reeds stand rimed ahead, and out of them runs the single line of big neat tracks, making for the pine'>>

choices.label: Follow the tracks' line off the ice to the pine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Off the ice through the far shore reeds, each stem cased in rime and chiming faintly, the tarn's white plate stretching back to the islet behind; ahead on its rise the second pine stands with its arms full of winter, and the ground beneath it holds its patient shadows'>>

choices.label: Up the rise to the pine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The knoll climb with the cache-house squatting at the top under its turf roof like a browsing beast, the guild's iron hasp bright with use against all the moor's abandonment; whoever else forgot this line, the autumn stocking crews did not; smoke-smell memory rises from the cold chimney stone'>>

choices.label: Up to the cache-house door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The stock shelves read like a guild sermon, oats, lamp-oil, wax, dry socks, tinned tallow, everything ranked and dated and mouse-proofed, the standing rule burned into the shelf edge, take what the route needs, note what you take, leave it better; the ledger's waxed wallet waits by the lamp'>>

choices.label: Open the ledger by the lamp.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The crest walked with the wind combing snow off the edge in white banners, the whole north country stepping up ridge by ridge toward the Queen's Ridge, which stands above them all with the weather smoking off its crown; the far loop's three tins hang polished and empty behind, and the question they ask walks along the crest too'>>

choices.label: Stop at the overlook and glass the moor below.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Where the line turns for the shelter stone the tracks part company at last, swinging away north with the same unhurried stride, toward the high ground; at the parting place they pause once, printed deeper, as if their maker stood a moment looking back along the route; then north, and gone; Bracken watches the way they went'>>

choices.label: Take the line's high turn for the shelter stone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The stone's lee out of the wind, the old fire-ring swept and the guild mark rechalked, the year added small to the chiselled tally of keepers; a route is a message, and this one reads again, end to end, in Maren's hand and one newer; the evening is going green and iron in the north'>>

choices.label: Turn for home down the finished line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Out on the Fellgate line while it still opens, the High Shoulder standing up ahead white on white with its zigzag wands like stitching up a sleeve; the errand rides urgent and the guild pace answers it, quick and steady and no quicker, because the mountain sets the terms; write this re-entry-safe for any state of the winter'>>

choices.label: Up to the foot of the shoulder.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='The seventh turn, where the valley lets go all at once and becomes a map of itself far below, villages pinned to it by their own smoke; the wind here has the crest's full voice and the wands lean hard-tested; breath, count, plant, climb, the drill carrying legs that would rather discuss the drop'>>

choices.label: Top out onto the cairn line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The cairns wear rime feathers a hand long, every one grown into the wind's eye like iron ferns, the guild's weather-sign that the crest has been colder longer than any valley knows; the fixed rope runs bright with use under its frost; ahead the line bends for the gap and the first far smell of quarry smoke'>>

choices.label: Along the last cairns to the gap.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The bore's far mouth, and Fellgate's stone bowl suddenly framed in it like a picture hung in the dark, terraces and smoke and the tarn galleries beyond, all seen from inside the hill the way the old cutters saw it; a strange proud way to arrive, through the mountain's own front door'>>

choices.label: Out and down toward the gap.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Down into the quarry street, and the silence has texture here, saws racked, sledge-chains hung, faces at windows counting who comes over the shoulder; a village that measures itself in stone knows exactly what a silent quarry weighs; the gatehouse door opens before the knock, as doors do on days like this'>>

choices.label: In to the quarrymaster's table.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The day-huts checked in passing, stoves cold, gear racked, the cutters' order holding everywhere like a signature; Osk reads each hut at a glance and moves on; not here, and not here, and the flag-line runs on toward the galleries with Bracken pulling sure and low on the lead'>>

choices.label: Follow the dog's line past the last hut.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Roping in at the gallery mouth in Brand's called order, lamps checked flame by flame, the rule said plain and short, rope-lengths apart, nobody out of sight, sing out early and often; a mountain rescue is mostly discipline wearing warm clothes, and this team wears it well'>>

choices.label: Into the galleries on the line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Out along the rim path guiding the second team, rope coils and the stretcher-board swinging in step, the pace disciplined to the slowest and the slowest apologising and nobody letting him; the galleries open ahead blue and ringing and the first team's lamps show in them like fish in ice'>>

choices.label: Join the search at the galleries.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The moraine path above the galleries, and the boot-sign confirming at every soft patch, two sets roped together, one favouring a foot, climbing deliberate and sensible toward the storm bothy; the search's lamps string out along the path and somebody ahead calls the smoke before Rill sees it'>>

choices.label: Up the last of the moraine to the bothy.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='The last steps down into the street with the chair-rig handed shoulder to shoulder, and Fellgate coming out of its doors in one long surge of lamplight and shouting relief; write this entry-agnostic, however the find was made the homecoming belongs to everyone now, and the street means to use all of it'>>

choices.label: Into the crowd and the counting.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=85 beats='Someone starts the old song about the white queen on the ridge, the one with the verses half the room half-remembers, and the gatehouse hall stumbles through it together, patching the gaps with humming; it is a kinder song than the fireside stories, older too, and it ends with a bowl set out in the snow, which nobody sings as a joke tonight'>>

choices.label: Slip out while the last verse settles.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='The beacon knoll beside the guildhall, and the muster gathering in the blue end of the day; write this arrival-neutral for any state of the winter and any road in, the scene is the same whenever the reader reaches it: yard hands take whatever the sledge carries the moment it halts, dogs are staked and watered, and Edda Vane stands at the knoll's stone table with the hearth-keepers' lanterns converging on her from the fell roads like slow deliberate stars'>>

choices.label: Join the circle at the stone table.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='The circle at the stone table: hearth-keepers and elders in from the villages by their own roads, runners, dogs, steam off tea in the lantern light; keep the recap path-universal, cite only what the whole winter has given everyone: the deep freeze hardening over the fells, the passes closing one by one, and from every quarter, by whoever carried it, the same word arriving all season, a white wolf on the high ground, old, thin, troubling no flock and no traveller, seen along the old lines'>>

choices.label: Listen as Edda lays the guild's evidence on the table.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Edda lays Maren's route-book and the guild's cache ledgersheets open on the stone: forty winters of entries in one steady hand, meat, marrow bones, tallow, carried to marked trees on every line of the fells; the tribute custom of the oldest stories, kept as plain maintenance by the old wayfinder and lapsed the winter she died; the hearth-keepers pass the pages hand to hand and the lanterns lean in, and nobody says nonsense, because the pages are in Maren's hand'>>

choices.label: Hear the council's question said out loud.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=105 beats='The council's question, argued the way villages argue, slow and in the round: some fear for flocks and children and want the high ground shunned till spring; some hear an old debt in the ledger's lists; the hearth-keepers speak for their own valleys in turn; Edda ends it the guild way, we do not decide about a thing we have not looked at, we go up together, by daylight rule and lantern, adults and runners, and we look, at distance, and then we decide'>>

choices.label: Stand with the muster: to the Queen's Ridge bearing.
Ask Edda outright what Maren would have done.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Edda turns the route-book to its last written page and does not answer in her own words at all; Maren's hand, small and exact: the custom is yours if you want it; she lets the page speak its piece to the lantern light and then closes the book gently, and that is Edda's whole answer, and the muster forms up'>>

choices.label: To the Queen's Ridge bearing with the muster.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='The bearing laid on the stone table's compass rose: every flag-line of the five fells, drawn out on the map, points its first wands at the same high ground, the Queen's Ridge, the way spokes point at a hub; the old surveyors built the routes outward from the ridge cache stones and no one alive remembered why until tonight; the muster takes the shared bearing north, lanterns in file, dogs at heel, the freeze squeaking underfoot'>>

choices.label: Up the beacon path in the lantern file.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='The lantern file on the beacon path, hooded flames swinging in step, breath pluming, the fells enormous and near under the early stars; hearth-keepers who have not walked together since the summer fair walk together now; behind and below, the villages keep their windows lit like people holding their breath politely; ahead the ridge stands black against the last green of the sky'>>

choices.label: Climb with the file to the shoulder of the ridge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The climb up the beacon path in called order, rope shared at the one steep step, lanterns handed hand to hand, the wind making its case and the muster making its slow answer; the weather is workable and being watched like a debtor; Edda sets the turning-back rule aloud for everyone, no shame in it, before the last pitch'>>

choices.label: Take the last pitch to the shelf below the crest.
The wind rises hard: stand with Edda as she calls the try.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='The shelf below the crest, out of the worst wind, the muster gathering its breath in a lantern half-ring; the youngest of the hearth-keepers' party is white-knuckled on her aunt's hand and pretending not to be; Edda hoods her own lantern and looks the party over, the glass in her mitt, the crest one easy rise above'>>

choices.label: Go up the last rise with Edda to look over the crest.
Take the frightened youngest's other hand and walk her down to the bothy light with her aunt.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Over the crest at a crawl, hooded lanterns doused, and there, by the great cache stone in the last grey light, she is: the White Wolf, the Wolf Queen of every fireside in the five fells, standing where the tribute stones have stood a thousand years; old, enormous, thin as a winter branch, her coat taking the dusk like snow takes it; she turns her head and looks down the ridge at the gathered small lights of them, and does not run, and no one breathes'>>

choices.label: Be absolutely still, and watch, as the guild watches.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='The long watching, at distance, adult and witnessed and reverent, the guild glass passed mitt to mitt down the half-ring; through it the truth arrives one detail at a time: the grey gone deep in her muzzle, the ribs under the white, the way she stands with her weight saved up like an old woman at a gate, and around her forefeet, pressed in the snow, the wide bare circle where she has waited at the empty cache stone many nights; no threat in her anywhere, only winter, and patience wearing thin as she is'>>

choices.label: Ease back off the crest with the muster to speak.
Volunteer to stay as first ridge-watch with old Sorrel and a shielded lantern.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Below the crest the muster speaks in low voices and the pieces close like a well-made box: she is real, she is the last of her line, and she has been walking the runners' routes all winter because the routes were built on her ground and an old wayfinder kept an old bargain at the marked trees until two winters ago; she has not been haunting the five fells, she has been keeping her side of a custom, and waiting for somebody to remember theirs; every face turns, the way faces do, to the one who wears Maren's guild patch'>>

choices.label: Step to the middle of the lantern ring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=115 beats='The lantern ring and the waiting fells, Edda's voice level: Maren's book says the custom is yours if you want it, so the guild asks its wayfinder's apprentice, what do we do with your grandmother's inheritance; write this arrival-neutral and let the weight sit plainly; the choices are all real and all kind, and one of them belongs to the runner who has carried the whole deep winter on their own sledge'>>

choices.label: Feed her: reopen Maren's cache line as the guild's winter tribute, starting tonight.
Ward her ground: lead the villages in closing the Queen's Ridge to traps and travel, a wide flagged way round.
Bring the full elder council of all five villages up to see her before anything is decided in her name.
Answer with the whole winter: stand as the runner who worked every deepening week of it, and take the custom up as your own.
Slip back with Bracken and let the elders decide; some answers want a night's sleep.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='The decision lands like a log on a fire, light everywhere at once: the guild stores are opened that same night, marrow bones and tallow and good meat weighed out by lantern, the hearth-keepers dividing Maren's list among their villages the way they divide any winter work; the sledge is loaded at the hall with the first tribute in two years, and the loading feels like mending something'>>

choices.label: Carry the first tribute out along Maren's marks with the muster.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='The first tribute laid at the near cache stones by lantern, adults and runners together, tins filled and pegged open, the snow smoothed, the party withdrawing to the flagged line the guild way, no lingering, no calling; from the dark high ground, presently, a shape comes down to the stones without hurry, and the watching lanterns hold their breath, and the sound that crosses the snow to them is only this: an old animal, eating, at peace'>>

choices.label: Stand with Edda while she writes the custom into the guild's standing routes.
Watch until she finishes and goes, and keep that sight for your own.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='The wardship drawn on the stone table's map that night: the Queen's Ridge and its approaches ringed in guild red, closed to traps, snares, and idle travel, a wide flagged way laid out around its skirts so no sledge or herd need cross her ground; each hearth-keeper signs for their own valley's edge of it, because a boundary is a promise made by everyone who lives along it'>>

choices.label: Walk the first stretch of the new way-round with the keepers at dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='The way-round flagged at first light, wands going in along the ridge's skirts while the fells turn rose and iron; hard clean work with the whole muster at it; from the crest, once, a pale shape watches the small figures fencing themselves out of her ground, wand by wand, and whatever a wolf understands of maps, she watches until the line is done and then beds down in the lee of the great stone like an approval'>>

choices.label: Stand at the last wand while Edda names the ground aloud.
Carry the signed map down to hang in the guildhall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='The word goes down the fell roads that same night, and the full elder council answers it: five villages' worth of grey heads and good boots on the beacon path at first light, roped and lanterned and grumbling happily, some of them last up this ridge as children; nothing is to be decided in her name, Rill has argued, by anyone who has not stood and seen her, and the fells' oldest people climb to do exactly that'>>

choices.label: Bring the council to the crest, hooded and quiet, the guild way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=105 beats='The council at the crest in the young light, and the White Wolf below at her stone, old and thin and past all denying; the effect on five villages' elders is the effect truth has on people who have argued about a rumour all winter: the arguing simply stops; someone starts the old song about the queen on the ridge, half under their breath, and does not get laughed at; the decision after this will belong to everyone, which was the whole point'>>

choices.label: Stand aside and let the council speak its mind up there.
Walk down with the elders and hear the telling begin at every hearth.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='Standing as the runner who worked the whole deepening winter, and the muster knows it because their own valleys kept the score; Edda opens the guild book on the stone table and reads the season's legs in order, each one a line in a runner's hand, and the iron-deep freeze standing over the fells tonight is the measure of how long and how faithfully the routes were kept open; then she takes Maren's route-book from her coat and does not hand it over so much as return it'>>

choices.label: Take the book, and say what the custom will be in your keeping.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=110 beats='The custom named in the lantern ring by its new keeper: the caches fed, the ground warded, the story told true at every hearth, all three, because Maren's line was never one errand but a way of holding five villages and one old queen in a single map; the hearth-keepers answer for their valleys one by one, aye, and aye, and aye, and aye, and aye, and the fells stand around the small lanterned circle like the oldest listeners at the fire'>>

choices.label: Sign the standing route in Maren's book beneath her last entry.
Climb to the crest once more and let her see the book-bearer plainly.
Hand the book's copy-work to the guild's youngest, and begin teaching.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='Down the beacon path with Bracken while the elders' voices go on quietly above, the lanterns of the muster a slow constellation behind; some answers do want a night's sleep, and a runner who has learned when to turn back has learned when to hand a question to more shoulders than their own; the guildhall windows rise up out of the dark like a bank of banked fires'>>

choices.label: In through the yard, dog fed first, the guild way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=95 beats='The hall at night-quiet, soup kept warm on the hook, Bracken asleep before the fire with his paws still walking some line in his dreams; through the window the ridge lanterns come down the fell in file, unhurried, which means the muster ended well; whatever the elders have decided, the fells will still need runners in the morning, and the morning is not far'>>

choices.label: Bank the fire and be asleep before the muster's boots reach the yard.
Wait up by the door to hear the decision from Edda herself.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=95 beats='Edda calls the try a hundred steps below the shelf, and no one argues with a guildmaster reading wind; the muster comes down in good order, lanterns counted, and the ridge keeps its secret one more night; at the hall Edda chalks tomorrow's try on the board before her coat is even off, because turning back and giving up are different words in the guild's language, and the first one is how the second never happens'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=95 beats='The walk down to the bothy light with the frightened youngest and her aunt, one small mittened hand in each of theirs, the crest and its answer left to others this night; the story of what stood by the cache stone arrives at the bothy later, in other voices, and loses nothing in the telling; some legs of a winter are about being the one who saw, and some are about being the hand somebody held, and the guild counts both in the book'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='The first ridge-watch kept with old Sorrel behind the shielded lantern, the muster's voices fading downhill, the White Wolf a pale patience at her stone under the sharpening stars; nothing happens, which is the point of a watch, and everything is seen, which is the other point; by morning the guild has its first true page about her nights, and Rill's hand wrote it, stiff-fingered, by a hooded flame'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='The custom goes into the guild's standing routes in Edda's plain hand, the tribute line, kept weekly, assigned like any other leg, and the first assignment sheet has Rill's name on it in the place of honour; what was a secret kindness of one old woman is now a duty of the whole guild, which is how kindnesses survive their keepers; on the high ground an old queen will eat this winter, and the five fells are warmer for knowing why'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='She feeds slowly, the way the old eat, and when she is done she stands a long moment over the tins with her head low, and then she looks down the dark at the watching lanterns, one long look, and goes up into the night ground that is hers; nobody speaks on the walk down; there are sights that turn into stories by morning, and sights that stay sights all your life, and Rill walks home carrying the second kind'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='At the last wand Edda names the ground aloud the old way, the Queen's Ground, hers as it was, and the keepers answer it valley by valley like a bell choir; the new way-round runs true and flagged and no longer trespasses on anyone; a map with mercy drawn into it, Maren would have said, is the only kind worth inking; the muster comes down with the sunrise on its shoulders and the fells feel wider and better ordered than they did last night'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='The signed map hung in the guildhall where every runner will pass it, five valley signatures around a red-ringed ridge and the plain words, closed ground, wide way flagged; not a story ending with trumpets, just a boundary that will hold because everyone who lives along it helped draw it; winters from now, runners who never saw her will keep the way-round without asking why, which is what a well-made rule is for'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='The council speaks its mind on the crest in the young light, low-voiced, bareheaded, five villages for once in one place and of one mind: she was here before the routes, the routes were laid kindly around her once, and they can be kind again; the decision is everyone's now, feed and ward both, carried by acclamation of the oldest voices in the fells, and Rill, who only asked them to look, gets the wayfinder's reward of watching people find the way themselves'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='Down the fell roads with the elders, and by dusk the telling has begun at every hearth in the five villages at once, the true version, old and thin and patient at her stone, not the fireside monster and not a fairy tale either; children who were afraid of the high passes ask to hear it twice; the Wolf Queen of the stories becomes, in one evening, the Wolf Queen of the neighbourhood, which is a different and better thing to be'>>

<<FILL role=success words=105 beats='The signature goes in beneath Maren's last entry, the same page, the custom is yours if you want it, and then, in a newer hand, wanted; Edda witnesses it the guild way and the hearth-keepers stamp their marks around it like a seal of five villages; the book goes back in the map room, but the line it holds goes back out into the fells, fed, warded, and told true, in the keeping of the wayfinder's heir, who has a dog to feed and a route to run in the morning'>>

<<FILL role=success words=105 beats='Once more to the crest, alone with Edda and the hooded lantern, and the old queen lifts her head at the small light and looks, long and level, at the one who carries the book now; nothing passes between them that a book could hold, and something passes all the same, the way it must have passed to Maren once, keeper to keeper, winter to winter; then she beds her head on her paws by the stone, easy as a hearth dog, and the new queen's-ward of the fells goes quietly down the mountain to begin'>>

<<FILL role=success words=100 beats='The copy-work handed to the guild's youngest apprentice at the hall table that very night, Maren's lists set out fair for small careful fingers to learn by, because Rill has understood the last thing the route-book teaches: a custom kept by one pair of hands died once already, and a custom taught keeps itself; the tribute line, the way-round, the true telling, all of it goes into young heads with the winter still deep, and the fells' oldest bargain gets what it never had before, a next hand, and a next'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='Asleep before the muster's boots reach the yard, dog-warm and bone-tired, and the decision arrives at breakfast with the porridge: the council will feed and ward both, and wants its runners rested; the winter goes on, the routes go on, and the high ground keeps its old queen with the whole five fells quietly in the know; not every keeper of a great night has to be awake at the end of it, and the morning legs still need fresh arms'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='Waiting up by the door when Edda's lantern crosses the yard, and hearing it from the guildmaster herself with the frost still on her collar: fed and warded, by everyone, beginning tomorrow; she looks at the apprentice a long moment and says only, Maren waited up for news the same way, and hangs her lantern, and that small sentence is worth the whole cold vigil; the fells settle for the night with their story finally true'>>
